situational_context: Running comeback week 6: Run #9 (5.79 km, Jun 10) completed — highest distance since comeback began May 3. ACWR elevated at 1.36 (above 1.3 cap) due to low chronic denominator (17.4) during rebuild — not genuine overload, confirmed by recovery markers. HRV returned to BALANCED today (34, weekly avg 35) after 10 days LOW/UNBALANCED (Jun 2-11), indicating autonomic adaptation absorbed. Body battery (55) and training readiness (76) at best levels in 10+ days — green light for moderate weekend sessions. Weight continuing gradual decline to 102.62 kg (-1.5 kg from May 14). Primary modalities: running 2x/week (comeback protocol, now at 4.5-5.8 km range) + e-bike commuting (~38 km round trip on sur site days). No new labs since Dec 2025; Vitamin D retest due ~July 2026. Young baby (Leonie, 5 months) — occasional sleep fragmentation but 7d sleep avg adequate at 7.5h.

recommendations.text: Hold run distance at 4.5 km for Run #10 (Sunday). ACWR at 1.36 demands consolidation, not progression. Let chronic load build over next 1-2 weeks before increasing to 5+ km again. Strict HR cap at 135 bpm — Run #9 drifted to avg 142 / max 173, contributing to the elevated suffer score.
recommendations.measurable: Run #10 completed at <=4.5 km with avg HR <=135 bpm and suffer score <=20

recommendations.text: Increase daily protein intake to >=150g (ideally 1.6 g/kg = 164g). Current 7d avg is ~127g — insufficient for tissue remodeling during running comeback at 102 kg. Prioritize protein at breakfast (currently averaging 350 kcal with low protein) and post-workout.
Maintain Vitamin D supplementation daily. Schedule blood panel for July 2026 (~8 weeks into comeback). Current insufficiency (27 ng/mL, Dec 2025) elevates stress fracture risk at this body weight.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Must drop below 1.3 before increasing run distance beyond 4.5 km
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 1.36; chronic load needs to catch up to acute. Expected to normalize within 1-2 weeks if load is held steady.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Monitor for 3+ consecutive BALANCED days to confirm recovery trend
markers_to_watch.reason: Today is first BALANCED reading in 10 days. Single day is not a trend — need confirmation before considering load increase.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Avg HR must stay <=135 bpm on Z2 runs; max HR <=160
markers_to_watch.reason: Run #9 showed avg 142 / max 173 — cardiac drift + heat. If repeated at 4.5 km distance, suggests pacing or heat management issue.
